SEARCH
Package io.github.dailystruggle.effectsapi.bukkit

Class BukkitValueCoercer

java.lang.Object
io.github.dailystruggle.effectsapi.bukkit.BukkitValueCoercer
All Implemented Interfaces:
ValueCoercer
public final class BukkitValueCoercer extends Object implements ValueCoercer
Bukkit-side implementation of ValueCoercer. Owns the leaf operations of effects-api-ADR-002's adaptive reading order (token → typed value), keeping effectsapi.common.Effect free of org.bukkit.* imports per effects-api-ADR-004.

The bodies of resolveSound, resolveNamedColor, and resolveViaRegistry were relocated here verbatim from the previous Effect.java so legacy behaviour is preserved exactly. The existing EffectsApiAdaptiveReadingOrderTest regression guard exercises the full ladder unchanged.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    canParse(TypeKey type, String raw)
    Cheapest, side-effect-free check: can the raw token be read as the given logical type? Must never throw.
    TypeKey
    classify(Object def)
    Map a defaults-map value to its logical TypeKey.
    Object
    parse(TypeKey type, String raw)
    Parse the raw token into the platform-native object the concrete effect consumes.
    List<TypeKey>
    readingOrder()
    Declared reading order for this platform.
    Object
    resolveReflective(Class<?> targetType, String raw)
    Reflective fallback used by Effect#fixData when ValueCoercer.classify(Object) returns TypeKey.UNKNOWN — the default value's runtime class is not one of the known logical types and the coercer should try valueOf(String) / getByName(String) / a registry lookup keyed by targetType.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • BukkitValueCoercer

      public BukkitValueCoercer()

  • Method Details

    • classify

      public TypeKey classify(Object def)
      Description copied from interface: ValueCoercer
      Map a defaults-map value to its logical TypeKey. Implementations should switch on Object.getClass() of def (and its fully-qualified class name when the platform type is registry-backed and not directly importable here).
      Specified by:
      classify in interface ValueCoercer
      Parameters:
      def - the default value, possibly null
      Returns:
      the logical type, never null; returns TypeKey.STRING for null (legacy "permissive" behaviour) and TypeKey.UNKNOWN for anything the coercer doesn't specifically understand

    • canParse

      public boolean canParse(TypeKey type, String raw)
      Description copied from interface: ValueCoercer
      Cheapest, side-effect-free check: can the raw token be read as the given logical type? Must never throw.
      Specified by:
      canParse in interface ValueCoercer

    • parse

      public Object parse(TypeKey type, String raw)
      Description copied from interface: ValueCoercer
      Parse the raw token into the platform-native object the concrete effect consumes. Throws IllegalArgumentException on parse failure (never returns null). Never performs chunk I/O (S-005).
      Specified by:
      parse in interface ValueCoercer

    • resolveReflective

      public Object resolveReflective(Class<?> targetType, String raw)
      Description copied from interface: ValueCoercer
      Reflective fallback used by Effect#fixData when ValueCoercer.classify(Object) returns TypeKey.UNKNOWN — the default value's runtime class is not one of the known logical types and the coercer should try valueOf(String) / getByName(String) / a registry lookup keyed by targetType.
      Specified by:
      resolveReflective in interface ValueCoercer
      Returns:
      the resolved object, or null when no resolver matches (caller surfaces an IllegalArgumentException per S-004)

    • readingOrder

      public List<TypeKey> readingOrder()
      Description copied from interface: ValueCoercer
      Declared reading order for this platform. ADR-002's ladder iterates defaults in declaration order; the platform's preferred order is informational and reserved for future cache-promotion tuning.
      Specified by:
      readingOrder in interface ValueCoercer
